title: Apparatus, system and method of communicating a single carrier (sc) space time block code (stbc) transmission
abstract: Some demonstrative embodiments include apparatuses, devices, systems and methods of communicating a Single Carrier (SC) Space Time Block Coding (STBC) transmission. For example, a station may generate a plurality of space-time streams including at least a first space- time stream and a second space-time stream, the first space-time stream including, in a first interval, a first data sequence followed by a first Guard Interval (GI) sequence, the second space- time stream including, in the first interval, a second data sequence followed by a second GI sequence, the first space time stream comprising, in a second interval subsequent to the first interval, a sign-inverted and time-inverted complex conjugate of the second data sequence followed by the first GI sequence, the second stream including, in the second interval, a time- inverted complex conjugate of the first data sequence followed by the second GI sequence; and transmit a SC STBC transmission based on the plurality of space-time streams.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus comprising logic and circuitry configured to cause a wireless station to: generate a plurality of space-time streams according to a Space Time Block Coding (STBC) structure, the plurality of space-time streams comprising at least a first space-time stream and a second space-time stream, the first space-time stream comprising, in a first interval, a first data sequence followed by a first Guard Interval (GI) sequence, the second space-time stream comprising, in the first interval, a second data sequence followed by a second GI sequence, the first space time stream comprising, in a second interval subsequent to the first interval, a sign-inverted and time-inverted complex conjugate of the second data sequence followed by the first GI sequence, the second stream comprising, in the second interval, a time- inverted complex conjugate of the first data sequence followed by the second GI sequence; and transmit a Single Carrier (SC) STBC transmission based on the plurality of space-time streams. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the first GI sequence is different from the second GI sequence. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein each of the first and second GI sequences has a length of M samples, and each of the first and second data sequences has a length of (N-M) samples, wherein N denotes a Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) size of each of the first and second intervals. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first GI sequence and the second GI sequence have a same length. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein each of the first and second GI sequences has a length of 32 samples or 64 samples. 6. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein each of the first and second GI sequences comprises a Golay sequence. 7. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of space-time streams comprises at least a third space-time stream and a fourth space-time stream, the third space-time stream comprising, in the first interval, a third data sequence followed by a third GI sequence, the fourth space-time stream comprising, in the first interval, a fourth data sequence followed by a fourth GI sequence, the third space time stream comprising, in the second interval, a sign-inverted and 

 time-inverted complex conjugate of the fourth data sequence followed by the third GI sequence, the fourth stream comprising, in the second interval, a time-inverted complex conjugate of the third data sequence followed by the fourth GI sequence. 8. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7 configured to cause the wireless station to convert the plurality of space-time streams into a respective plurality of frequency-domain streams in a frequency domain, and to map the plurality of frequency domain streams to a plurality of frequency domain spatial streams according to an STBC scheme. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the STBC scheme comprises an Alamouti scheme. 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7 configured to cause the wireless station to transmit a first spatial stream of the SC STBC transmission via a first antenna and a second spatial stream of the SC STBC transmission via a second antenna. 11. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the SC STBC transmission comprises an NTXNR SC transmission, wherein ÎÏ is an integer equal to or greater than 2, and NR is an integer equal to or greater than 1. 12. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the first and second intervals comprise Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) intervals. 13. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7 configured to cause the wireless station to transmit the SC STBC transmission over a Directional Multi-Gigabit (DMG) band. 14. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the wireless station is a Directional Multi-Gigabit (DMG) Station (STA). 15. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7 comprising a plurality of directional antennas to transmit the SC STBC transmission. 16. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7 comprising a radio, a memory, and a processor. 17. A product comprising one or more tangible computer-readable non-transitory storage media comprising computer-executable instructions operable to, when executed by at least one computer processor, enable the at least one computer processor to cause a wireless station to: generate a plurality of space-time streams according to a Space Time Block Coding (STBC) structure, the plurality of space-time streams comprising at least a first space-time stream and a second space-time stream, the first space-time stream comprising, in a first interval, 

 a first data sequence followed by a first Guard Interval (GI) sequence, the second space-time stream comprising, in the first interval, a second data sequence followed by a second GI sequence, the first space time stream comprising, in a second interval subsequent to the first interval, a sign-inverted and time-inverted complex conjugate of the second data sequence followed by the first GI sequence, the second stream comprising, in the second interval, a time- inverted complex conjugate of the first data sequence followed by the second GI sequence; and transmit a Single Carrier (SC) STBC transmission based on the plurality of space-time streams. 18. The product of claim 17, wherein each of the first and second GI sequences has a length of M samples, and each of the first and second data sequences has a length of (N-M) samples, wherein N denotes a Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) size of each of the first and second intervals. 19. A method to be performed at a wireless station, the method comprising: processing a Single Carrier (SC) Space Time Block Coding (STBC) transmission received via one or more antennas of the wireless station; and demodulating the SC STBC transmission according to a STBC structure of a plurality of streams of the SC STBC transmission, the plurality of streams comprising at least a first space-time stream and a second space-time stream, the first space-time stream comprising, in a first interval, a first data sequence followed by a first Guard Interval (GI) sequence, the second space-time stream comprising, in the first interval, a second data sequence followed by a second GI sequence, the first space time stream comprising, in a second interval subsequent to the first interval, a sign-inverted and time-inverted complex conjugate of the second data sequence followed by the first GI sequence, the second stream comprising, in the second interval, a time- inverted complex conjugate of the first data sequence followed by the second GI sequence. 20. The method of claim 19 comprising demodulating the SC STBC transmission according to a Linear Minimum Mean Square Error (LMMSE) scheme. 21. The method of claim 19, wherein each of the first and second GI sequences has a length of M samples, and each of the first and second data sequences has a length of (N-M) samples, wherein N denotes a Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) size of each of the first and second intervals. 22. The method of claim 19, wherein the plurality of streams comprises at least a third space-time stream and a fourth space-time stream, the third space-time stream comprising, in the 

 first interval, a third data sequence followed by a third GI sequence, the fourth space-time stream comprising, in the first interval, a fourth data sequence followed by a fourth GI sequence, the third space time stream comprising, in the second interval, a sign-inverted and time-inverted complex conjugate of the fourth data sequence followed by the third GI sequence, the fourth stream comprising, in the second interval, a time-inverted complex conjugate of the third data sequence followed by the fourth GI sequence. 23. The method of claim 19, wherein the first and second intervals comprise Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) intervals. 24. The method of claim 19 comprising receiving the SC STBC transmission over a Directional Multi-Gigabit (DMG) band. 25. A product comprising one or more tangible computer-readable non-transitory storage media comprising computer-executable instructions operable to, when executed by at least one computer processor, enable the at least one computer processor to cause a wireless station to perform the method of any one of claims 19-24.
